# AI-Generated Optimization Tips:
### Cursor IDE Rules for Efficient Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview Usage

To optimize the use of Google Lyria 3 Pro Preview for coding tasks while minimizing token usage and cost, the following five practical Cursor IDE rules can be implemented. These rules focus on prompt engineering techniques to ensure efficient and cost-effective interaction with the model.

#### Rule 1: Specify the Programming Language and Task

```json
{
  "name": "Language and Task Specification",
  "pattern": "^([a-zA-Z]+) ([a-zA-Z]+)",
  "action": {
    "type": "insert",
    "value": "Write a $1 program to $2. Use the most efficient approach and provide explanations for the code."
  },
  "description": "Ensures that the model understands the context and provides relevant, efficient code."
}
```

**Example:** Typing "Python sorting" will be expanded to "Write a Python program to sorting. Use the most efficient approach and provide explanations for the code."

#### Rule 2: Focus on Functionality Over Implementation Details

```json
{
  "name": "Functionality Over Details",
  "pattern": "^how (to|do) ([a-zA-Z ]+)",
  "action": {
    "type": "insert",
    "value": "What is the most efficient way to $3 in terms of functionality? Provide a high-level overview."
  },
  "description": "Prioritizes understanding the functionality over implementation specifics, reducing unnecessary detail."
}
```

**Example:** Typing "how to sort a list" will be expanded to "What is the most efficient way to sort a list in terms of functionality? Provide a high-level overview."

```json

  "name": "Minimal Viable Code",
  "pattern": "^example ([a-zA-Z]+) ([a-zA-Z]+)",
